item.title: Broward Transitional Center — Pompano Beach FL (GEO Group)
item.description: 600-bed GEO Group facility in Pompano Beach FL running at 105-108% capacity with $42.9M/year contract. 911 calls doubled in 2025; one death in custody; 80% of detainees have no criminal record.

item.title: Eloy Detention Center — Pinal County AZ (CoreCivic)
item.description: 1,550-bed CoreCivic facility in Eloy AZ called the ‘deadliest ICE detention center’ with 16 deaths including 5 suicides. Part of the 11,200+ bed Pinal County CoreCivic cluster.

item.title: Eloy Federal Contract Fac — Pinal, AZ
item.description: IGSA detention facility in Pinal, AZ.

item.title: Florence Service Processing Center — Pinal County AZ (ICE / Akima Global Services)
item.description: ICE-operated processing center in Florence AZ with guard/food services contracted to Akima Global (Alaska Native Corp) at $37.4M/year via sole-source 8(a) contracts escalating 12.5% over 3 years.

item.title: Folkston D Ray ICE Processing Ctr — Charlton, GA
item.description: IGSA detention facility in Charlton, GA.

item.title: Folkston ICE Processing Center — Charlton County GA (ICA / D. Ray James Merger)
item.description: 3,000-bed GEO Group facility (merged with D. Ray James) in Charlton County GA – nation’s largest ICE detention center. 16 deaths, systemic violations, and economic capture of a 12,000-person county.

item.description: IGSA detention facility in Charlton, GA.

item.title: La Palma Correctional Center — Eloy AZ (CoreCivic)
item.description: 3,060-bed CoreCivic facility in Eloy AZ converted from California state prisoners to ICE use in 2019. DHS OIG found abuse and mistreatment; staff deployed chemical agents on COVID protesters in 2020.

item.title: Webb County Detention Center — Laredo TX (CoreCivic)
item.description: CoreCivic-operated ICE detention facility in Laredo, TX under IGSA, on the I-35 corridor at the busiest US-Mexico commercial crossing. ANC score of 3 signals heavy alternatives-to-detention contracting.

item.title: Webb County Detention Center (CCA) — Webb, TX
item.description: IGSA detention facility in Webb, TX.

item.title: West Baton Rouge Det Ctr — West Baton Rouge, LA
item.description: IGSA detention facility in West Baton Rouge, LA.

item.description: IGSA detention facility in Kenai Peninsula, AK.

item.description: IGSA detention facility in Kenai Peninsula, AK.

item.description: IGSA detention facility in Yavapai, AZ.

item.title: York Corr Inst — New London, CT
item.description: IGSA detention facility in New London, CT.

item.title: York County Jail — York, ME
item.description: IGSA detention facility in York, ME.

item.title: York County Jail, PA — York, PA
item.description: IGSA detention facility in York, PA.

item.title: Yuba County Jail — Yuba, CA
item.description: IGSA detention facility in Yuba, CA.

item.title: Zavala County Jail — Zavala, TX
item.description: IGSA detention facility in Zavala, TX.

item.title: Roxbury NJ Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: $129.3M warehouse (470K sq ft) in Roxbury NJ purchased at 137% markup from Goldman Sachs subsidiary. Governor, AG, and township jointly sued; bipartisan backlash in NJ.

item.title: Salt Lake City UT — ICE Mega Center (833,000 sq ft warehouse)
item.description: $145.4M purchase of 833K sq ft warehouse in Salt Lake City for 7,500-10,000 bed mega center. Sold by Deutsche Bank subsidiary at 50%+ markup; city imposed water restrictions to resist.

item.title: Social Circle GA Warehouse — ICE Mega Detention Center
item.description: $129M warehouse in Social Circle GA (valued at $29M, 333% markup) planned as 7,500-10,000 bed mega center. City locked water meter because 1M gal/day sewage demand exceeds town’s 660K capacity.

item.title: Socorro TX Warehouse — ICE Processing Center (~1000% Markup)
item.description: ICE warehouse in Socorro, TX (El Paso County) purchased for $123M despite $11M prior valuation, a ~1000% markup and the most extreme overpayment documented in the ICE warehouse program.

item.title: Surprise AZ Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: ICE warehouse in Surprise, AZ (Maricopa County), 550 beds, purchased for $70M vs $12M prior value (~500% markup). Contractor awarded the project has no detention background.

item.title: Tremont PA Warehouse — ICE Mega Detention Center
item.description: Former Big Lots warehouse in Tremont, PA (Schuylkill County), 7,500-10,000 beds, bought from Blue Owl Capital for $119M (~2x market value). 33 Trump officials hold Blue Owl investments.

item.title: Williamsport MD — 1,500-Bed ICE Detention Center (Goldman Sachs / Fundrise)
item.description: Vacant warehouse in Williamsport, MD, 1,500 beds, owned by Fundrise and refinanced by Goldman Sachs months before DHS purchase. Judge extended restraining order against operations.

item.title: Alexandria Staging Facility — ICE's Deportation Flight Cornerstone
item.description: 400-bed GEO Group facility on a former Air Force Base tarmac in Alexandria LA, cycling 1,000+ people weekly. Cornerstone of ICE deportation flight operations and largest ICE Air hub.

item.title: Alligator Alcatraz — South Florida Detention Facility (Dade-Collier)
item.description: 3,000-5,000 bed state-operated facility built in 8 days in the Florida Everglades, opened July 2025. Documented abuse, maggot-infested food, and legal challenges; costs $450M/year to operate.

item.title: Baker County Detention Facility — North Florida 'Deportation Depot
item.description: 1,300+ bed county jail in Sanderson FL (former state prison), opened Aug 2025 with GEO Group management. Part of Florida’s IGSA detention network; dubbed ‘Deportation Depot’ by protesters.

item.title: Bradford County ICE Detention Campus (Douglas Building)
item.description: Proposed 3,000-bed IGSA facility in Starke FL on a contaminated 30-acre site, phased from 1,000 to 3,000 beds at $221-269/bed/day. Pitched by Sabot Consulting as county-led economic development.

item.title: California City Immigration Processing Center (CoreCivic)
item.description: 2,560-bed CoreCivic facility in Kern County CA generating ~$100M/year under a two-year ICE contract. One of the largest private detention centers in the country, reactivated Aug 2025.

item.title: Camp Atterbury IN — Military ICE Detention
item.description: Indiana National Guard base ~40 miles south of Indianapolis designated for up to 1,000 ICE detainees. Named in Defense Secretary Hegseth’s July 2025 letter; still in planning phase.

item.title: Camp Blanding FL — 'Deportation Depot' ICE Detention Center
item.description: Florida National Guard base near Starke planned for 2,000 ICE detainees, branded ‘Deportation Depot’ by DeSantis. Paused until Alligator Alcatraz fills up; state-operated model.

item.title: Camp East Montana — Fort Bliss ICE Tent Detention Camp
item.description: 3,000+ bed tent camp on Fort Bliss in El Paso TX, opened Aug 2025 under a $1.2B Army contract. Three deaths in custody, tuberculosis cases, and documented abuse; operator being replaced.

item.title: Central Louisiana ICE Processing Center, Jena LA
item.description: 1,160-bed GEO Group processing center in Jena LA, part of the Louisiana detention hub with Alexandria Staging Facility. Notable for holding detained college students including Mahmoud Khalil.

item.title: Delaney Hall — Newark NJ GEO Group ICE Facility
item.description: 1,000-bed GEO Group facility in Newark NJ reactivated in 2025 under a ~$1B contract. Part of GEO’s expansion from 20,000 to 26,000 ICE beds driving record $254M profit in 2025.

item.title: El Paso TX Eastwind Logistics Center — ICE Mega Detention Center
item.description: $123M warehouse purchase in Far East El Paso County planned as 8,500-bed mega center. Three warehouse buildings totaling ~888K sq ft; faces water infrastructure limits.

item.title: Glades County Detention Center — Moore Haven FL
item.description: 500-bed county jail in Moore Haven FL with IGSA reinstated April 2025. Part of Florida’s county jail detention network; a 19-year-old detainee death drew media attention.

item.title: Guantanamo Bay Migrant Operations Center (GMOC)
item.description: Trump ordered GMOC expansion from 130 to 30,000 capacity in Jan 2025, but fewer than 400 held at any time. Places immigration detainees outside normal federal court reach at estimated $40M/month.

item.title: Hagerstown MD Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: ICE purchased a Hagerstown MD warehouse for $100M+ in Jan 2026 as one of 16 regional processing centers. Part of the $38.3B Detention Reengineering Initiative targeting FY2026 completion.

item.title: Hamburg PA — ICE Processing Center (warehouse purchase)
item.description: Warehouse in Berks County PA purchased as one of 16 ICE regional processing centers with 1,000-1,500 bed capacity. Part of the Pennsylvania detention cluster with Upper Bern and Tremont.

item.title: Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst NJ — Military ICE Detention
item.description: Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst in NJ approved for up to 1,000 ICE detainees per Hegseth’s July 2025 letter. Governor Murphy opposed; no firm timeframe. NJ faces double expansion with Roxbury warehouse.

item.title: Kansas City MO — ICE Mega Center (BLOCKED by community pressure)
item.description: Planned 7,500-bed mega center in south Kansas City blocked when Platform Ventures withdrew after mass protests, student walkouts, and Port KC severing ties. Key seller-pressure resistance model.

item.title: Merrimack NH Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: 43-acre warehouse in Merrimack NH planned as 500-1,500 bed ICE processing center at $300M estimated cost. Paused after community opposition in this swing-state town; ACLU obtained leaked ICE site plans.

item.title: Midwest Regional Reception Center — Leavenworth KS (CoreCivic)
item.description: 1,033-bed former federal prison in Leavenworth KS awarded to CoreCivic via no-bid contract. Delayed by zoning challenge; excluded from CoreCivic’s 2026 financial projections due to uncertainty.

item.title: North Lake Processing Center — Baldwin MI (GEO Group)
item.description: 1,800-bed GEO Group facility in Baldwin MI reactivated in 2025. Described as ’notorious’ for conditions; part of GEO’s expansion adding 6,000 beds to its ICE capacity.

item.title: Romulus MI Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: 500-bed ICE warehouse at 7525 Cogswell St, Romulus MI purchased Feb 2026 without public notice. Near schools and in a floodplain; Michigan AG and city filed lawsuit to block conversion.

item.title: San Antonio TX East Side Warehouse — ICE Processing Center
item.description: $66M+ warehouse (640K sq ft) on San Antonio’s East Side planned as 1,500-bed ICE processing center. Assessed value was $37M; part of the 24-warehouse Detention Reengineering Initiative.

item.title: Upper Bern Township PA Warehouse — ICE Processing Center
item.description: ICE processing center in Upper Bern Township, PA (Berks County), purchased for $87M in Feb 2026. Legal questions raised about local authority to block federal detention facilities.
